artifacts.claimText: Iowa State guidance says confidential data classified as moderate or above may not be entered into any generative AI product unless assessed and approved under ISU data classification and applicable compliance processes.

artifacts.claimText: Iowa State's Office of the Vice President for Research states that ISU does not have a formal policy regarding AI use in research and instead urges principal investigators to follow guidance from professional societies, funding agencies, and publishers.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI Guidance - Iowa State Research
citation.publisher: Iowa State University Office of the Vice President for Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Guidance - Iowa State Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: No. With a technology that is evolving as rapidly as GenAI, any formal policy is likely to already be outdated by the time it would be enacted across the institution. Therefore, we urge all principal investigators to follow the AI guidance and direction of their professional societies, funding agencies, and the publishing houses they target for their research papers.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Does ISU have a formal policy regarding AI use in research?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.publisher: Iowa State University Office of the Vice President for Research

artifacts.notes: Approved as a research-specific source-status claim; it does not claim there is no central AI guidance outside research.

artifacts.claimText: Iowa State research guidance warns that users cannot assume GenAI tools comply with HIPAA, FERPA, and similar confidentiality rules, and says uploading information to a public AI tool is equivalent to releasing it publicly.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI Guidance - Iowa State Research
citation.publisher: Iowa State University Office of the Vice President for Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Guidance - Iowa State Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You cannot assume GenAI tools are compliant with rules and laws designed to ensure the confidentiality of private information, such as HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996) and FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act). Uploading information (e.g., research data, grant proposals, unpublished manuscripts, or analytical results) to a public AI tool is equivalent to releasing it publicly.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What are the risks of uploading my research information to a GenAI system?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Faculty and Staff - Office of Student Conduct
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty and instructors may wish to add specific information in their syllabus related to use of AI content generation. CELT has developed recommendations for syllabus language about AI that may be useful in addressing these concerns, and provides information on potential alternative assignments and assessments. Suspected AI-generated coursework referrals will be reviewed similar to any other form of academic misconduct.

artifacts.title: Iowa State University staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Official Iowa State sources support staged claims that the central AI site lists available tools and data-protection status; policies/compliance guidance restricts moderate-or-above confidential data in generative AI unless assessed and approved and requires compliance with laws and university policies; research guidance states there is no formal research AI policy and warns about public AI disclosure risks; CELT provides teaching resources; Student Conduct treats suspected AI-generated coursework referrals similarly to other academic misconduct; and Marcom guidance restricts AI-only marketing materials, requires human review, and bars confidential data in generative AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not published canonical records.
The ITS committee page was used for discovery context only, not policy evidence.
The linked Canvas course page was not fetched because authenticated access may be required.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
